title: Preemptive Pharmacovigilance — Three-Tier Signal Detection
description: Proactive safety signal detection beyond reactive PRR. Three tiers: Reactive (standard disproportionality), Predictive (severity-weighted PRR), Preemptive (Gibbs thermodynamic energy modeling). Plus trajectory amplification, noise floor correction, and competitive inhibition intervention modeling. Pure Rust via nexcore-preemptive-pv.

tools.name: reactive
tools.description: Tier 1: Standard reactive signal detection from a 2x2 contingency table. Returns PRR, chi-squared, and detection verdict.

tools.name: predictive
tools.description: Tier 2: Severity-weighted predictive detection. Multiplies PRR by seriousness omega weight (ICH E2A criteria). More serious events get amplified signals.

parameters.name: seriousness
parameters.description: non_serious, hospitalization, disability, life_threatening, or fatal

tools.name: gibbs
tools.description: Tier 3: Preemptive detection via Gibbs free energy thermodynamic model. Negative delta-G = spontaneous signal (drug-event association is thermodynamically favorable). Models signal as chemical reaction equilibrium.

tools.name: evaluate
tools.description: Full three-tier evaluation: runs reactive, predictive, AND preemptive detection in one call. Returns results from all tiers plus overall tier classification and recommendation.

tools.name: trajectory
tools.description: Trajectory amplification: detect accelerating reporting trends using Hill curve amplification on time-series data. Reports slope, amplified slope, and whether the signal is accelerating.

tools.name: severity
tools.description: Get the severity weight (omega) for a seriousness level. Returns the ICH E2A-based weight used in predictive and preemptive tiers.

parameters.name: seriousness
parameters.description: non_serious, hospitalization, disability, life_threatening, or fatal

tools.name: noise
tools.description: Noise floor correction: compute corrected signal by subtracting background noise and testing statistical significance.

tools.name: intervention
tools.description: Model competitive inhibition intervention: compute residual signal after applying an intervention of given strength. Based on enzyme inhibition kinetics.

tools.name: required-strength
tools.description: Compute the intervention strength needed to achieve a target signal reduction.

tools.name: omega-table
tools.description: Reference table of all seriousness omega weights used in predictive and preemptive tiers.
